title: aita - feeling like mil wants me to go to work for selfish reasons
text: so i’m a new mum to a beautiful baby boy - my partner and i are the first on both sides to have kids so it’s been a lot to take in for everyone.  it was an unexpected pregnancy so we were definitely scrambling in those 9 months to save up for me to go on maternity leave for a few months at least and then return to work. well my mil straight away quit her job to do part time and offered to look after our son once i go back to work. while i was grateful i wasn’t sure when that would be which i mentioned a few times. a little backstory about her - she’s very distant with her kids and before our son was born she never really spent time with my husband or me. she just kind of had her own life and then when our son was born she suddenly was in the picture (which kind of hurt my husband because he’s always wanted a close family). the other thing is she is very passive aggressive and gives me that “yeah whatever look” any time i try to let her know how what our sons routine is, when he goes to bed, or how to settle him. and she leaves our house as soon as i need to breastfeed him and gets very defensive if he gets restless when she’s holding him. she’s also very jealous of my mum and how close we are. anyways before i was due i was fired (for being pregnant wtf) so going back to work seemed daunting to me - i was anxious about going back to work with a new baby in case i was fired again for being a mum. so my husband and i decided to budget and allow me to stay home till the end of the year or until a job that works for us pops up. well my mil keeps nagging about when i’ll go back to work and seems really annoyed that “she’s changed her work” in order to look after our son. i feel like telling her that i didn’t ask for her to do that and now i just feel upset and pressured into going back to work again - like i’m doing the wrong thing. every time she comes over she mentions it and has even gone so far as mention that my son might get too clingy. i just feel like she wants to look after our son without me around because she doesn’t like being in my company...

storyboard.content: I’m a new mum to a baby boy. It was an unexpected pregnancy, so my partner and I were scrambling to save up for me to go on maternity leave.
My mother-in-law immediately quit her job to go part-time and offered to look after our son once I go back to work.
I was grateful but told her several times I wasn’t sure when I would be returning to work.

My MIL has always been very distant with her kids and never really spent time with my husband or me before our son was born. She suddenly became involved once the baby arrived, which hurt my husband because he’s always wanted a close family.

She is very passive aggressive and dismissive whenever I try to explain our son’s routine, when he goes to bed, or how to settle him. She leaves the house as soon as I need to breastfeed and gets defensive if the baby gets restless when she holds him.
Before I was due, I was fired from my job for being pregnant. This made going back to work feel daunting because I was anxious about being fired again for being a mum.
My husband and I decided to budget so I could stay home until the end of the year or until a suitable job comes up.

My MIL keeps nagging about when I’ll go back to work and seems really annoyed that she changed her work schedule to look after our son. Every time she comes over she mentions it, and has even said my son might get too clingy.

I didn’t ask her to change her work. I feel upset and pressured into going back to work, like I’m doing the wrong thing. I think she wants to look after our son without me around because she doesn’t like being in my company.
